title: Additive manufacturing inside the human eye
abstract: Additive manufacturing techniques are used to form an artificial intra-ocular lens (IOL) directly inside the human eye. Small openings are formed in the cornea and lens capsule of the eye, and the crystalline lens is broken up and removed through the openings; then, a material is injected into the lens capsule through the openings, and the focal spot of a pulse laser beam is scanned in a defined pattern in the lens capsule, to transform the material in the vicinity of the lase focal spot to form the IOL in a layer-by-layer manner. In one embodiment, stereolithography techniques are used where a pulse UV laser source is used to photosolidify a photopolymer resin. The liquefied resin is injected into the eye through the openings, after which only part of the resin, having the shape of the desired IOL, is selectively cured with the UV laser beam, via progressive layer formation.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of treating a patient's eye, comprising: using a pulse laser beam from a laser system to form an opening in a cornea and an opening in a lens capsule of the patient's eye; using the pulse laser beam from the laser system to break up a crystalline lens of the eye from the lens capsule; removing the broken up crystalline lens through the openings in the cornea and the lens capsule; injecting a material into the lens capsule through the openings in the cornea and the lens capsule; and forming an artificial IOL inside the lens capsule by scanning a focal spot of the pulse laser beam from the laser system inside the lens capsule in a defined scanning pattern to transform the material in a vicinity of the focal spot of the pulse laser beam. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the material is a polymer material, and wherein the injected polymer material forms a volume of the polymer material inside the lens capsule. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the forming step includes: adjusting a depth of the focal spot of the pulse laser beam within the volume of the polymer material; scanning the focal spot of the pulse laser beam in a lateral plane at the adjusted depth across a defined area; and repeating the adjusting step and the scanning step. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein the polymer material includes a liquefied photopolymer resin, and wherein the liquefied photopolymer resin is solidified in the vicinity of the focal spot of the pulse laser beam. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the liquefied photopolymer resin includes a water- soluble crosslinkable poly(vinyl alcohol) prepolymer, and wherein the pulse laser beam is a UV pulse laser beam. 6. The method of claim 2, wherein the polymer material includes a polymer powder, and wherein the polymer powder is fused together in the vicinity of the focal spot of the pulse laser beam. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the polymer powder includes an acrylic powder, and wherein the pulse laser beam is a UV pulse laser beam. 8. The method of claim 2, further comprising, after the forming step: removing untransformed polymer material from the lens capsule. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the material includes unassembled individual slices of the IOL, wherein the injecting step includes incrementally injecting the individual slices into the lens capsule, and wherein the forming step includes scanning the focal spot of the pulse laser beam to incrementally assemble injected individual slices together. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the individual slices include slices of polymethyl methacrylate, and wherein the forming step includes scanning the focal spot of the pulse laser beam to melt surface layers of the polymethyl methacrylate slices. 11. A method for forming an artificial intra-ocular lens (IOL) inside a patient' s eye, comprising: injecting a material into a lens capsule of the patient's eye, where a natural lens of the eye has been removed from the lens capsule; and forming an artificial IOL inside the lens capsule by scanning a focal spot of the pulse laser beam from the laser system inside the lens capsule in a defined scanning pattern to transform the material in a vicinity of the focal spot of the pulse laser beam. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the material is a polymer material, and wherein the injected polymer material forms a volume of the polymer material inside the lens capsule. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the forming step includes: adjusting a depth of the focal spot of the pulse laser beam within the volume of the polymer material; scanning the focal spot of the pulse laser beam in a lateral plane at the adjusted depth across a defined area; and repeating the adjusting step and the scanning step. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the polymer material includes a liquefied photopolymer resin, and wherein the liquefied photopolymer resin is solidified in the vicinity of the focal spot of the pulse laser beam. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the liquefied photopolymer resin includes a water- soluble crosslinkable poly(vinyl alcohol) prepolymer, and wherein the pulse laser beam is a UV pulse laser beam. 16. The method of claim 12, wherein the polymer material includes a polymer powder, and wherein the polymer powder is fused together in the vicinity of the focal spot of the pulse laser beam. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the polymer powder includes an acrylic powder, and wherein the pulse laser beam is a UV pulse laser beam. 18. The method of claim 12, further comprising, after the forming step: removing untransformed polymer material from the lens capsule. 19. The method of claim 11, wherein the material includes unassembled individual slices of the IOL, wherein the injecting step includes incrementally injecting the individual slices into the lens capsule, and wherein the forming step includes scanning the focal spot of the pulse laser beam to incrementally assemble injected individual slices together. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the individual slices include slices of polymethyl methacrylate, and wherein the forming step includes scanning the focal spot of the pulse laser beam to melt surface layers of the polymethyl methacrylate slices.
